Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class RandomFloatingBusProvider

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Floating bus provider that returns random values for each read.

public sealed class RandomFloatingBusProvider : IFloatingBusProvider
Inheritance
object
RandomFloatingBusProvider
Implements
IFloatingBusProvider
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a randomized floating bus implementation that simulates unpredictable bus noise, useful for testing software robustness and detecting code that incorrectly relies on specific floating bus values.

Behavior: Returns a random byte value (0x00-0xFF) for each read operation, simulating the unpredictable nature of reading from unmapped memory addresses where the data bus may contain arbitrary values from previous bus activity.

Use Cases:

  • Testing - Verifies software doesn't depend on specific floating bus values
  • Debugging - Helps identify uninitialized memory reads or bus conflicts
  • Robustness validation - Ensures code handles arbitrary floating bus data
  • Copy protection analysis - Some protection schemes test for random vs. deterministic behavior

Limitations: This implementation is not accurate to Apple II hardware behavior. Real hardware floating bus values are not truly random - they reflect the last value on the data bus (often video data during screen refresh). Software that relies on specific floating bus patterns (video synchronization, timing loops, some copy protection) will not work correctly. For production use, consider LastValueFloatingBus or CycleAccurateFloatingBus.

Randomness: Uses Random with a time-based seed for non-cryptographic randomness. Each instance maintains its own random number generator to ensure deterministic behavior within a single emulator session if needed.

Performance: Slightly slower than NullFloatingBusProvider due to random number generation overhead, but still negligible compared to other emulation operations. The random number generator is instance-scoped to avoid thread-safety issues.

Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Each instance should be used by a single thread (typically the emulator worker thread). The internal Random instance is not synchronized for performance reasons.

Constructors

RandomFloatingBusProvider()

Initializes a new instance of the RandomFloatingBusProvider class.

public RandomFloatingBusProvider()

Remarks

Creates a new random number generator with a time-based seed. Each instance will produce a different sequence of values, making each emulator session unique.

RandomFloatingBusProvider(int)

Initializes a new instance of the RandomFloatingBusProvider class with a specific seed.

public RandomFloatingBusProvider(int seed)

Parameters

seed int

Seed value for the random number generator.

Remarks

Using a specific seed allows for reproducible behavior across multiple runs, which is useful for:

  • Debugging - Reproduce specific floating bus sequences that trigger bugs
  • Testing - Ensure consistent test results across runs
  • Save states - Maintain consistent floating bus behavior when loading saved state

Example: 

// Create deterministic floating bus provider for testing
var floatingBus = new RandomFloatingBusProvider(12345);

// Same seed produces same sequence
var floatingBus2 = new RandomFloatingBusProvider(12345);
Assert.Equal(floatingBus.Read(), floatingBus2.Read());

Methods

Read()

Reads the floating bus value (returns a random byte).

public byte Read()

Returns

byte

A random byte value between 0x00 and 0xFF.

Remarks

Each call returns a new random value. This simulates the unpredictable nature of reading from unmapped memory where the data bus contains arbitrary values.

Distribution: Uses uniform distribution across the full byte range (0-255). All values have equal probability.

Performance: Generates a random integer and masks to byte range. Typical execution time is ~10-20ns on modern processors.
